TSLA: Tesla says FSD Supervised is available in China (TSLA:NASDAQ). Tesla has announced the availability of its FSD (fully self-driving) Supervised system in China, adding it to a list of other countries where it is already offered, including the U.S. and Canada. The timeline for the launch of the fully autonomous FSD system in China, however, remains uncertain. This supervised version requires active monitoring by a human driver.

Analyst Predicts a 2027 Merger Between Tesla and SpaceX. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives predicts a merger between Tesla and SpaceX in 2027, citing Tesla's existing stake in SpaceX and their joint Terafab facility. Ives believes Elon Musk aims to control more of the AI ecosystem by combining the two disruptive tech companies. SpaceX's prospectus also acknowledges a strong partnership through commercial engagements.

Barclays (DJP) offers $300K buffered callable notes linked to TSLA, COIN, HOOD. Barclays Bank PLC is offering $300,000 in Buffered Callable Contingent Coupon Notes due May 23, 2029, linked to the performance of TSLA, COIN, and HOOD. These notes pay a contingent coupon of $19.583 per $1,000 (23.50% per annum) if each underlying asset meets its coupon barrier. Principal repayment at maturity depends on the least performing asset's final value relative to its buffer value, with potential losses up to 60%.
TGT: Walmart Inc. $WMT Shares Bought by ABN Amro Investment Solutions. ABN Amro Investment Solutions significantly increased its stake in Walmart Inc. (WMT) during the fourth quarter, boosting its holdings by 239.9% to 204,499 shares valued at approximately $22.8 million. This comes as Walmart continues to receive bullish analyst attention, with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average target price of $138.88. The company has demonstrated strong operating momentum, consistently beating EPS and revenue estimates, and analysts are closely watching its upcoming earnings report for indications of consumer resilience and potential further stock appreciation.

Analyst Stock Picks: Target, Sea Limited, JD.com in Focus. Wall Street analysts are focusing on key single-name stock ideas across retail, Southeast Asian tech, and AI infrastructure this week, including Target (TGT), Sea Limited (SE), and JD.com (JD). These discussions come amid weakening global consumer sentiment and elevated oil prices, prompting investors to look for defensive names and growth stories with reasonable valuations. Lumentum (LITE) is also highlighted as a beneficiary of AI infrastructure demand.

Kroger Co. stock (US5010441013): Analyst sentiment and valuation in focus after fresh institutional buying. Kroger Co. stock is gaining attention due to new institutional buying and updated analyst data. The North Dakota State Investment Board recently initiated a $1.3 million position, and 16 brokerages rate the stock a "Moderate Buy" with an average 12-month price target of $74.87. While some valuations suggest potential undervaluation based on earnings, other metrics indicate the shares might be overvalued, leading to a mixed outlook for long-term predictability.

Tapestry, Deckers, and Travel + Leisure Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know. Shares of Tapestry, Deckers, and Travel + Leisure jumped after major retailers reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter earnings, signaling a potential turn in consumer discretionary momentum. The positive results, particularly from discount-oriented retailers like Target, suggest that American household spending is more resilient than analysts anticipated, despite inflation. Tapestry's shares are up 5.2% since the beginning of the year, although they are still trading below their 52-week high.

Tapestry, Deckers, and Travel + Leisure Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know. Shares of Tapestry, Deckers, and Travel + Leisure jumped after major retailers like Target, Lowe's, and TJX reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter earnings, signaling a potential recovery in consumer discretionary spending. These positive reports suggest that American household spending is more resilient than analysts anticipated, with consumers still spending despite inflation, particularly on value-oriented goods. Tapestry, in particular, has seen a 5.2% increase year-to-date, despite a recent dip due to inflation concerns.

Williams-Sonoma and Sally Beauty Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know. Williams-Sonoma and Sally Beauty shares jumped after major retailers like Target, Lowe's, and TJX reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings, signaling resilience in consumer spending. This positive trend, particularly from discount retailers, suggests consumers are still spending despite inflation, especially on value-oriented products. The article also notes Sally Beauty's volatility and its previous drop due to a higher-than-expected Producer Price Index report.

Nasdaq Gains 100 Points; Target Earnings Top Views. U.S. stocks saw mixed trading with the Nasdaq Composite gaining 100 points, while the Dow was down and the S&P 500 rose. Target Corp (NYSE: TGT) reported strong first-quarter earnings and raised its FY2026 sales forecast, beating analyst expectations. Several individual stocks experienced significant movements, with Silexion Therapeutics Corp, GCL Global Holdings Ltd, and Capstone Holding Corp shares rising, and Jayud Global Logistics Ltd, 707 Cayman Holdings Ltd, and Lichen International Ltd shares falling.

Stronger Q1 lifts Target (NYSE: TGT) sales 6.7% and EPS outlook. Target Corporation reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with net sales increasing 6.7% to $25.4 billion and comparable sales growing 5.6%. The company raised its full-year 2026 guidance, now expecting net sales growth of approximately 4% and GAAP and Adjusted EPS near the high end of its previous range of $7.50 to $8.50. Despite a GAAP EPS decline due to prior-year one-time gains, Adjusted EPS rose 31.6% to $1.71, reflecting improved underlying profitability.

Target Earnings: Traffic Surges, but Recovery Still Demands Heavy Investment. Target's first-quarter results showed a 5.6% increase in comparable sales and a 32% rise in adjusted EPS, driven by successful category resets and operational improvements. Despite these positive indicators and a 4.4% traffic increase, the company faces an intensely competitive retail landscape and macroeconomic pressures on consumers. Target plans significant investments in grocery and beauty, but its midmarket positioning and reliance on heavy reinvestment keep its long-term sales growth forecast low.
SBUX: Global Partners taps Starbucks vet to lead retail operations. Global Partners has appointed Emily Filc, a veteran from Starbucks with 22 years of experience, as its new vice president of retail operations. Filc, who previously oversaw 140 Starbucks locations and led numerous pilot programs, will help enhance guest experiences and operational discipline across Global Partners' retail network. This hiring aligns with Global Partners' increased investment in its convenience retail network, including opening new Alltown Fresh and Honey Farms Market locations, with planned capital expenditures of $75 million to $85 million this year for facility development and upgrades.

Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) settles cash tender offers for senior notes. Starbucks Corporation has completed cash tender offers for several series of its senior notes, reducing portions of its outstanding debt. The company accepted specific amounts of notes, including 4.800% Senior Notes due 2030 and 5.400% Senior Notes due 2035, while other series remained unchanged. Starbucks settled on the Early Settlement Date and does not plan to purchase additional notes through these offers, thus reshaping its debt profile.
DIS: Envestnet Portfolio Solutions Inc. Acquires 21,594 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Envestnet Portfolio Solutions Inc. increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) by 38.5% in the fourth quarter, acquiring an additional 21,594 shares to hold a total of 77,741 shares valued at $8.85 million. Other institutional investors also modified their holdings, reflecting strong overall institutional interest with hedge funds and institutions owning 65.71% of the stock. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Disney with a consensus price target of $134.47, following the company's recent earnings beat of $1.57 EPS on $25.17 billion in revenue.

Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co. Cuts Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co. significantly reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 52.5% in the fourth quarter, selling over 151,000 shares. Despite this, Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Disney, with an average price target of $134.47, following recent upgrades and a better-than-expected earnings report. The article also touches upon positive park developments and ongoing legal risks related to a facial-recognition lawsuit.
NKE: McDonald’s Laces Up with Basketball Star Devin Booker to Debut the Nike Book 2 McDonald’s Sneaker. McDonald's is collaborating with NBA star Devin Booker to launch the limited-edition Nike Book 2 McDonald's sneaker. These sneakers, inspired by Booker's Arizona roots and a unique McDonald's location with turquoise arches in Sedona, will be available in two colorways. Fans can enter a sweepstakes via the McDonald's app for a Friends & Family edition or purchase a general release version on Nike.com starting June 2.
